channel.title: shell on David Nguyen's Personal Blog
channel.description: Recent content in shell on David Nguyen's Personal Blog

item.title: Improve productivity with fzf
item.description: As a developer, I spend a lot of time on command line and since I found fzf, a command line fuzzy finder, I find my productivity has been improved tremendously by applying fzf in everday commands that I use. Below is the collection of small fzf snippets that I wrote and use a lot myself. fignore: Download .gitignore Usage Source 1 2 3 4 curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/github/gitignore/git/trees/master?recursive=1 | \ jq -r '.

item.title: Speed up ZSH loading of custom functions
item.description: I have been using zsh with Prezto for a few years and have always been conscious about what stuffs to put in my shell profile files like .zshrc so that a new shell can start as fast as it can. If comparing the startup time of my shell to shell startup time of most other people who I have worked with, my current startup time is not bad: In average, it takes around 0.
